messages.subject: [R-gui] RKWard 0.4.2
messages.body_plain:
Dear R-SIG-GUI subscribers,

I have not announced any new versions of RKWard on this list for quite a long 
time (in fact, a far as I can see, the last time was more than two years ago, 
for version 0.2.0).

For those, who have never seen it: RKWard is a KDE GUI for R. As such, it is 
currently a Linux/BSD/... only application. Once KDE 4 will be released, it 
will be portable to Windows, too.

For those, who have not tried it during the past few months: We'd like to 
encourage you to give it a try. As signified by the version number, RKWard is 
still far from finished, but we feel it is already highly useful as an 
advanced IDE for R.

Current features include:
* An auto-updating object browser, allowing you to explore the entire R 
workspace, including loading packages
* An R console (actually an emulation) including syntax highlighting, Tab code 
completion, and function argument hinting
* A script editor based on the powerful and feature rich Kate. Also with 
syntax hightlighting, code completion, and argument hinting, as well as code 
folding
* Integrated editor for data.frames
* Integrated help browser and help search
* X11 device windows are managed by the GUI
* Multiple document interface: Several data editor, script editors, help 
windows can be opened at the same time
* Asynchronous command execution: Run lengthy calculations in R, and continue 
working on your data / scripts at the same time
* Log of all R commands
* R library management
* Extensible using plugins, and shipped with plugins providing basic data 
import / export, data analysis, plotting and distribution functions

Regards
Thomas Friedrichsmeier

Regards
Thomas Friedrichsmeier

messages.subject: [R-gui] Tinn-R 1.19.0.14 released
messages.body_plain:
Dear Tinn-R user,

We would like to announce that a new version of the Tinn-R for Windows
(1.19.0.14) was released and is available on SourceForge server:
http://sourceforge.net/projects/tinn-r

Tinn-R is a R script editor under OS Windows and we thinking no more release new 
versions in the current year.

Changes:
1.19.0.14 (Dec/06/2006)

- 'Search text' and 'Replace text' interface were reworked.

- Some parts of the source code were optimized.

- It is now possible to increase and decrease (nonpermanent) the font size of
   any individual file easily:

   SHIFT + CTRL + (plus  (+) on the numeric keypad): INCREASE (limit is 50)
   SHIFT + CTRL + (minus (-) on the numeric keypad): DECREASE (limit is  1)

Regards,
Tinn-R team
